title: HLR comply with system requirements
formulation_paraphrase: Every high-level requirement must derive from or trace to an allocated system requirement

title: HLR accurate and consistent
formulation_paraphrase: High-level requirements are accurate / unambiguous / sufficiently detailed and conflicts are resolved
template_params: node=HLR; necessary-condition proxy = no high-severity Smell; residual judgment on semantic accuracy
notes_disputed: DISPUTED-resolved: smell screen is necessary-not-sufficient -> hybrid, not machine. Canonical proxy-screen example.

title: HLR compatible with target computer
formulation_paraphrase: Performance/timing/memory HLR must be linked to target hardware characteristics
template_params: node=HLR filtered category in {Performance,Timing,Memory}; require outgoing CONSTRAINED_BY -> HardwareCharacteristic
notes_disputed: BORDERLINE: existence of link is machine; whether the constraint value is actually compatible is human. Kept machine (trusted metadata) per DR-zone rule.

title: LLR comply with HLR
formulation_paraphrase: Every low-level requirement is traceable to at least one parent high-level requirement

title: Architecture consistent with HLR
formulation_paraphrase: Every HLR is realized by at least one implementing module (software or HDL)
template_params: node=HLR; require incoming IMPLEMENTS from SoftwareModule|HDLModule

title: Test procedures correct (every req tested)
formulation_paraphrase: Every HLR and LLR has at least one verifying test case
template_params: node=Requirement filtered level in {HLR,LLR}; require incoming VERIFIES from TestCase
notes_disputed: BORDERLINE: existence of a test is machine; correctness of the procedure content is human. Kept machine per DR-zone rule.

title: Test coverage of HLR (MC/DC for DAL A)
formulation_paraphrase: DAL-A HLR require a VERIFIES edge whose coverage_type is MC/DC
template_params: node=HLR filtered dal=A; require incoming VERIFIES where edge.coverage_type='MC/DC'
notes_disputed: TRUST-BOUNDARY EXEMPLAR: graph checks the asserted MC/DC metadata, NOT the actual coverage measurement (done by a qualified coverage analyzer). Machine given trusted evidence.

title: HWR verifiability - method assigned
formulation_paraphrase: Every hardware requirement has an assigned verification method
template_params: node=HWR filtered dal in {A,B,C}; require outgoing VERIFIED_BY_METHOD -> VerificationMethod
notes_disputed: *Section numbering is OUR RECONSTRUCTION - DO-254 has no Annex-A-style objective table. Verify against standard text.

title: HWR traceable to system or derived w/ rationale
formulation_paraphrase: HWR either traces to a system requirement OR is derived with documented rationale
template_params: disjunctive obligation: (trace edge) OR (is_derived=true AND outgoing DERIVED_FROM_RATIONALE -> Rationale)
notes_disputed: *Reconstructed numbering. First disjunctive instance - composes T1 with a guard clause.

title: Derived requirements documented w/ rationale
formulation_paraphrase: Derived hardware requirements must carry a documented rationale
template_params: node=HWR filtered is_derived=true; require outgoing DERIVED_FROM_RATIONALE -> Rationale
notes_disputed: *Reconstructed numbering. The further 'validated against safety analysis' clause is human -> the codified part is the rationale-existence subclaim only.

title: Derived requirements safety impact assessed
formulation_paraphrase: Derived DAL-A/B hardware requirements must have a safety impact assessment
template_params: node=HWR filtered is_derived=true & dal in {A,B}; require outgoing SAFETY_ASSESSED_BY -> SafetyAnalysis
notes_disputed: *Reconstructed numbering. Existence of an assessment is machine; adequacy of the assessment is human.

title: Verification compliant - passing artifact
formulation_paraphrase: DAL-A/B hardware requirements must have a passing test or a proven assertion
template_params: node=HWR filtered dal in {A,B}; require incoming VERIFIES from TestCase{status=passed} OR Assertion{status=proven}
notes_disputed: *Reconstructed numbering. Node-status qualifier - same template family as A7-3 edge qualifier.

title: HW/SW interface coherent
formulation_paraphrase: SW HLR and HW HWR referencing the same interface must assert mutual consistency
template_params: (HLR)-MENTIONS->(iface)<-MENTIONS-(HWR) with NO CONSISTENT_WITH edge between HLR and HWR
notes_disputed: Detects MISSING consistency assertion (screening), not semantic consistency of the interface itself.

title: Timing budget consistent SW+HW
formulation_paraphrase: Sum of child SW and HW timing budgets must not exceed the system-level budget
template_params: aggregate: SUM(children.timing_budget_ns) <= parent.timing_budget_ns over DERIVES_FROM children
notes_disputed: Fully machine - numeric, no judgment. The class single-domain tools cannot detect.

title: Hazard mitigated on both SW and HW
formulation_paraphrase: Catastrophic/hazardous dual-domain hazards must have mitigation requirements on BOTH sides
template_params: node=SafetyHazard filtered severity in {catastrophic,hazardous} & mitigation_domain in {both,sw_and_hw}; require incoming MITIGATES from HLR AND from HWR
notes_disputed: The 'where applicable' scoping (mitigation_domain field) is a human-set input - machine check is conditional on it.
